goal: Strategy doc + BUILD-PLAN.md + compound beads initialization
AN Score Spec v0.1 + Namespace Packet
Live audit findings → journey fixes + human→agent content surfaces

note: Shipped local SQLite durable event outbox for billing/audit streams, app startup wiring, and risky-write health gating across execute paths.

summary: Hardened Resolve v2 dogfood loop bootstrap path so unattended Keel runs no longer depend on a stale long-lived dogfood key.

Shipped the first live AUD-18 PostgreSQL direct read wedge through execute plus capability discovery/resolve surfaces, with a seed migration and focused tests, while keeping credential truth honest as BYOK-only.
Shipped a repeatable AUD-18 live DB dogfood harness and boosted Postgres/SQL capability search ranking after the first public connection_ref proof exposed a discovery weakness.

Hardened the AUD-18 hosted DB agent_vault rail so early DB execute failures now emit receipts and capability_executions records with real status codes instead of falling out before provenance surfaces.
Re-ran live hosted AUD-18 DB failure proofs on api.rhumb.dev and confirmed all five early-failure cases now return the expected honest errors after the DB receipt/provenance hardening slice.

Shipped the tighter AUD-18 connection_ref provenance branch so DB success responses, failure envelopes, and human-readable summaries now carry the non-secret ref when available, narrowing the remaining hosted DB hardening gap to a stronger vault-backed bridge.
Attempted live hosted proof of the new signed DB agent-vault bridge and confirmed the blocker is deploy truth, not code truth: api.rhumb.dev still returns 404 on /v1/db/agent-vault/tokenize.

Confirmed the hosted signed DB agent-vault route is live and mints tokens, but the execute proof is still blocked by proof-material truth: available candidate DSNs do not form a working PostgreSQL login for bounded dogfood proofing.

Applied the production 0161 object-storage capability seed so S3 capabilities resolve live, then confirmed the remaining blocker is deploy truth: hosted execute still falls through the old generic BYO proxy rail instead of the new direct storage path.

Repaired stale dogfood auth for aws-s3, confirmed the hosted direct S3 execute path is live, and narrowed the remaining blocker to missing hosted storage_ref config after all bounded proof calls returned storage_ref_invalid on the direct rail.

Shipped a repeatable hosted S3 dogfood harness at scripts/s3_read_dogfood.py and reran the bounded proof bundle. The immediate rerun cleared a transient first-request 401 and confirmed the current blocker is still just missing hosted storage_ref config, with all six checks failing honestly on storage_ref_invalid.

Narrowed the hosted S3 blocker further: the direct rail and dogfood auth are live, but there is still no real object-storage credential bundle to attach because no S3/R2 keys exist in 1Password and the available Cloudflare account does not have R2 enabled.

Shipped S3 storage bundle bootstrap tooling in ad75051: scripts/build_s3_storage_bundle.py now generates and validates RHUMB_STORAGE_<REF> payloads and prints the exact Railway set command, while docs/API.md now documents the S3 rail and proof flow. Live blocker remains real AWS credentials plus hosted RHUMB_STORAGE_ST_DOCS config.

Shipped AUD-18 S3 endpoint override support in 8924b09 so runtime storage bundles now honor optional endpoint_url; added focused tests plus API contract docs; next blocker remains sourcing a real bounded S3-compatible credential bundle and setting RHUMB_STORAGE_ST_DOCS.

Closed the WU-T2 dogfood evidence gate after the first natural Keel run under the widened 900s timeout landed green, refreshed fleet status back to ok_profiles=3/3, and returned the active build loop to AUD-18 S3 hosted proof closeout.

Shipped repeatable AUD-18 support proof-source audit tooling so Zendesk credential discovery no longer depends on ad hoc sweeps. scripts/audit_support_proof_sources.py now scans shared vault metadata, the rhumb browser profile, and Gmail metadata into a single artifact; the first run confirms the blocker is still credential truth, not missing code, because no vault-backed Zendesk bundle or non-public workspace evidence exists locally.

Created the first operator-owned Intercom admin login for AUD-18, then fixed scripts/audit_support_proof_sources.py so vault presence only counts as proof-ready when a candidate item actually contains the scoped bundle fields required for hosted proof. The refreshed Intercom source probe now stays honest: candidate login exists, but bearer-token + region + scope material is still missing.
